mission.title: containerd: Containers with curl based health checks becoming unresponsive
mission.description: Containers with curl based health checks becoming unresponsive. This issue affects 27+ users.

steps.title: Identify containerd troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your containerd deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd -n containerd --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `context deadline exceeded"``

steps.title: Review containerd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant containerd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
kubectl get configmap -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=containerd
```
**Description**

After containerd update to 1.4.0, running a Docker container with a health check using curl (specifically bitwardenrs/server and plex images) will result in an 'unhealthy' status after 15 minutes or so.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Containers with curl based health checks becoming…
steps.description:
Docker hardcodes shim v1.
This will be fixed in Docker 20.x.

--- EDIT fix typo
```yaml
"Log": [
  {
    "Output": "OCI runtime exec failed: exec failed: cannot exec a container that has stopped: unknown"
  }
```

steps.title: Confirm Containers with curl based health checks becoming… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd -n containerd --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n containerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `context deadline exceeded"`` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
Docker hardcodes shim v1.
This will be fixed in Docker 20.x.

--- EDIT fix typo
codeSnippets: level=warning msg="runtime v1 is deprecated since containerd v1.4, consider using runtime v2"

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with containerd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
